note: BRAIN layer — Day 10 value delivery queue. CRITICAL: Claire call is TODAY at 14:00. All audit tasks should be complete before 13:30 at the latest. Rob (lead 22) nudge due EOD if no response. Tom (lead 1) nudge due tomorrow EOD.

items.new_pain_point_discovered: No online booking system — self-reported lost customers
items.reply_summary: Confirmed Tuesday 2pm call. Volunteered booking gap as biggest issue.

audit_tasks.notes: Fresha (free for salon, per-booking fee to customers) vs Calendly (simple embed). Key sell: Fresha is sector-specific, handles deposits, reminders, repeat bookings. Calendly simpler but less powerful. Recommend Fresha.

option_a.name: Quick Fix
option_a.notes: Present as baseline — position as 'what you'd need as a minimum'. Not the natural recommendation.

option_b.name: Full Fix — Recommended
option_b.scope: SSL + mobile + Fresha booking widget integration + above-fold CTA rewrite
option_b.notes: Lead with this. Claire self-identified the booking gap — validate her instinct, then solve it. Fresha is free for the salon. Zero monthly fee for her — only customers pay a small booking fee. Present as obvious choice.

call_script_notes: OPEN: 'Claire — you mentioned in your email that you lose customers because there's no way to book online. Tell me a bit more about how that plays out in a typical week.' Let her describe the pain in her own words.
VALIDATE: 'That's exactly what I see on the site — visitors can't book, so they either call or give up. The ones who give up often end up booking a competitor.'
COMPETITOR INSERT: Use lead_3_competitors.json — name one or two Leeds salons ranking above her that DO have online booking. Make it tangible.
SOLUTION: 'There are two ways to approach this...' — present Option A and Option B clearly. Pause after each. Do not rush.
CLOSE: 'Which of those feels like the better fit for you?' — let her answer. Do not talk over the silence.
IF SHE HESITATES: 'I can send a one-page summary so you can look it over — I'll have it with you within the hour.'
DO NOT: Mention AI, automation, or your tools. Keep framing around her business outcomes only.
post_call_actions: If she commits on the call: send a brief written confirmation of scope and price within 2 hours. Request 50% upfront payment via bank transfer.
If she wants to think it over: send proposal PDF (Option A and B clearly laid out) within 30 minutes of call ending. Follow up Thursday if no response.
If she declines: thank her, leave door open, mark lead 3 as LOST.

items.reply_summary: Asked whether SSL/mobile can be bundled into full redesign. 'Sort the whole thing in one go' — upsell signal.

option_b.name: Full Redesign Bundle
option_b.scope: New design + SSL + mobile + contact form + portfolio gallery

option_c.name: Redesign + Ongoing SEO
option_c.scope: Option B + monthly local SEO ('print shop Sowerby Bridge')

items.nudge_trigger: No response from Rob by 17:00 TODAY (2026-04-07) → send fu_022_ROB_NUDGE: 'Happy to send a one-page scope doc if that makes it easier to confirm, Rob.'
items.notes: Rob signalled bundle intent — do NOT anchor down to Option A. His question was commercial ('sort the whole thing'), not cost-driven. The scope doc offer removes decision friction without reducing price.

items.new_pain_point_discovered: No online quote form — customers told to ring instead
items.reply_summary: Acknowledged the quote gap, said he's been meaning to sort it. Asked for pricing.

audit_tasks.notes: 1-2 examples of local trades businesses with good quote request forms — sent with pricing reply.

items.nudge_trigger: No call booking or reply from Tom by 17:00 TOMORROW (2026-04-08) → send fu_026_TOM_NUDGE: 'Tom — happy to jump on a 10-min call Wednesday morning to confirm the spec.'
items.call_booking_note: If Tom responds today and wants to book: offer Thursday or Friday slot — NOT Tuesday (Claire's call day) and not Wednesday morning if that's reserved. Confirm immediately.

if_claire_converts.action: Update lead 3 status → converted. Log revenue in analytics. Create delivery task. Send invoice/bank transfer request same day.
if_claire_converts.note: Write a brief note in CHANGELOG.md for the first conversion — it's a milestone.

if_claire_defers.action: Send proposal PDF within 30 mins of call ending. Follow up Thursday 2026-04-10 if no response.
if_claire_defers.note: Do not chase before Thursday. One clear follow-up only.

if_claire_declines.action: Mark lead 3 LOST. Log in CRM. No further contact unless she re-initiates.
if_claire_declines.note: Rare outcome given the call booking — but plan for it.

summary.pipeline_value_estimate: £800-1,200 if all three convert (lead 3: £350, lead 22: £550-675, lead 1: £100-150)
summary.expected_this_week: Lead 3 likely to convert today or Thursday. Lead 22 scoping in progress — nudge today if silent.
summary.gdpr_note: All three leads replied voluntarily to cold outreach — legitimate interest basis confirmed. Interactions logged in audit_log.
